style: Off-dry to medium-sweet, high acidity, low alcohol (8-9%), floral and citrus aromas. Pale lemon colour, stone fruit and green apple notes.
common_student_errors: Marking acidity as medium because sweetness softens perception
Missing balance between sweetness and acidity

misconceptions_addressed.misconception: Sweet wine always means low quality
misconceptions_addressed.correction: Off-dry Riesling with high acidity and complexity demonstrates that sweetness is a style choice, not a quality marker
Low alcohol Riesling can show great complexity, finesse and ageing potential
High acidity in Riesling counterbalances residual sugar and is critical to quality assessment

coaching_hints: Are the sweetness and acidity in balance?
How does acidity support quality despite the residual sugar?
What indicates ageing potential in this sweet wine?
Notice the low alcohol — what does this tell you about the vintage/harvest timing?

causal_reasoning_paths.effect: High acidity retained; low sugar accumulation; low alcohol results
causal_reasoning_paths.example_in_wine: Off-dry style with 8-9% alcohol and high acidity

causal_reasoning_paths.cause: Harvest timing (botrytis risk without full noble rot)
causal_reasoning_paths.effect: Residual sugar balanced by natural acidity
Mineral character and stone fruit aromatics
